info.description: This problem set focuses on viral pathogens and antiviral agents. The difficulty may vary a lot through out the set, since this set contains a mix of four different cognitive levels of complexity (recall, understand, apply, analyze). The questions are already reviewed, so mistakes should have already been minimized, but if you come across one, you can report through the Forms in the "Info and Links".

q1.prompt: A 4-year-old unvaccinated child develops fever, sore throat, and malaise followed by asymmetric flaccid paralysis of the lower limbs without sensory loss. What is the mode of transmission of the viral causative agent in this patient?

q2.prompt: Upon a direct examaination of a tissue sample, an inclusion body called molluscum bodies are found. The agent that causes the formation of these inclusion bodies is in the same family as which of the following viruses?

q3.prompt: Hepatitis B virus (HBV) utilizes a unique replication strategy among DNA viruses. Following the entry of the viral genome into the host nucleus and the production of mRNA, which enzymatic process is required to generate the DNA genome of progeny virions?

options.label: Rolling circle replication occurring in the host cytoplasm
Direct translation of the DNA genome by host cell ribosomes
Integration of the viral DNA into the host chromosome via integrase
Semiconservative DNA replication by host cell DNA polymerase
Reverse transcription of a pregenomic RNA intermediate into DNA

q4.prompt: A 5-year-old child presents with a high fever, cough, and conjunctivitis. During the physical examination, the clinician observes small, bright red spots with blue-white centers on the buccal mucosa. This pathognomonic clinical finding is termed what?

q5.prompt: A 5-year-old child presents with a high fever, cough, and conjunctivitis. During the physical examination, the clinician observes small, bright red spots with blue-white centers on the buccal mucosa. This pathognomonic clinical finding is associated with which viral infection?

q6.prompt: Favipiravir is an antiviral medication utilized in the treatment of various RNA viruses, including SARS-CoV-2 and certain influenza strains. What is the primary mechanism of action for the active triphosphate form of this drug?

options.label: Interference with the host cell's ribosomal protein synthesis
Blockade of viral attachment to the host cell membrane
Prevention of mature virion release from the cell surface
Inhibition of the viral RNA-dependent RNA polymerase
Competitive inhibition of the viral integrase enzyme

q7.prompt: A 19-year-old patient presents with painful vesicular lesions on the genitalia. A Tzanck smear prepared from the lesion reveals the presence of multinucleated giant cells. This microscopic finding confirms an infection by a member of which viral family?

q8.prompt: In the replication cycle of HIV, the viral enzyme integrase is responsible for a critical step following the synthesis of double-stranded DNA. What is the primary function of this enzyme?

options.label: Facilitating the uncoating of the viral core in the cytoplasm
Inserting the linear viral DNA into the host cell chromosome
Promoting the budding of new viral particles from the membrane
Cleaving large viral polyproteins into functional subunits
Synthesizing a DNA copy from the viral RNA template

q9.prompt: The process of "reassortment," which can lead to major antigenic shifts and global pandemics, occurs when two different viral strains infect the same cell and exchange genetic material. This process is possible only in viruses that possess which of the following genomic features?

q10.prompt: In a laboratory experiment, a cell is simultaneously infected with Virus A and Virus B. During the assembly phase, the genome of Virus A is packaged within a protein coat (capsid/envelope) derived entirely from Virus B. What determines the tropism (the ability to infect a specific cell type) of this resulting hybrid progeny?

options.label: Reassortment of genomic segments between both viruses
The protein coat derived from Virus B
The site of viral replication in the cytoplasm
The genetic material derived from Virus A
